diagrams.title: Peterson's Solution Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a process using Peterson's solution with all sections clearly labeled
elements: Remainder Section (RS) - Normal processing outside critical section
Entry Section (ES) - Where process requests and waits for entry
Critical Section (CS) - Where shared resource is accessed
Exit Section (ES) - Where process signals exit from critical section
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the four sections of each process and where shared variables are used

diagrams.title: Peterson's Solution State Diagram
diagrams.description: Visual representation of state transitions in Peterson's solution
elements: State 1: Both flag false - Neither wants to enter
State 4: Both want in - turn determines winner
State 5: P0 in CS, P1 waiting
State 6: P1 in CS, P0 waiting
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how processes transition between states based on flag and turn values

diagrams.title: Entry Section Logic Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step decision flow in the entry section of Peterson's algorithm
elements: Step 1: Set flag[i] = true (I want to enter)
Step 2: Set turn = j (You can go if you want)
Step 3: Check: Is other process wanting to enter? AND Is it other's turn?
Step 4: If YES to both in step 3, wait (busy-wait)
Step 5: If NO to either condition, proceed to Critical Section
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the exact logic of the entry section and why it ensures mutual exclusion
